weather_report:
Jolimont settles under an unbroken ceiling of cloud tonight, the sky a seamless slate that swallows the stars and smooths the edges of the streets. At this late hour it feels quiet and enclosed rather than stark; light pools softly beneath the lamps and the night keeps its voice down. There’s no sun to speak of and, with overcast lingering, the UV is effectively nil; clarity is muted, the world rendered in calm, patient shades.

That same blanket looks set to carry into the early hours, so dawn should arrive subdued, with little sparkle and only a shy chance of brighter breaks later on. Expect flat, even light through the morning; UV should stay low at first, nudging up only if the ceiling thins toward midday. Dress in uncomplicated layers with a light jacket; sunglasses can wait in the pocket. At this hour, a quiet stretch or laying out tomorrow’s kit suits the mood; come morning, a slow walk for bread, a simple errand, or a window seat with coffee feels just right.
